title: Framework Sync Rush
description: You stabilize a failing networked-aframe framework by memorizing and replaying linked pulse routes before the whole structure desyncs.

howToPlayJson.title: Framework Sync Rush
howToPlayJson.objective: Replay the glowing pulse path across a small stack of connected A-Frames, keep the framework online for a 2-4 minute run, and bank the highest sync score on the leaderboard.

controls.input: Tap the lit A-Frame nodes in the exact order shown
controls.outcome: Confirms each beat, advances the sequence, and builds combo

controls.input: Tap the flashing link brace before replay starts
controls.outcome: Switches the next pattern into Mirror Mode, reversing the sequence for a score multiplier

controls.input: Hold a desynced node for a short press-and-hold when it crackles red
controls.outcome: Clears the jammed node but costs a beat of timer pressure

steps: A short audiovisual pulse sequence travels through 3 connected A-Frame nodes.
The sequence ends and the player must tap the same nodes back in order.
Each clear adds one new beat, speeds up playback slightly, and raises the combo multiplier.
Every few rounds, one link brace flashes and offers Mirror Mode: replay the whole next sequence in reverse for bonus points.
Later rounds introduce desynced red nodes that mute their sound cue, forcing the player to rely on visual memory unless they spend time to
A missed tap, wrong order input, or timeout breaks the framework and ends the run immediately.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score comes from correct beats, end-of-sequence clears, and a rising combo multiplier that grows with consecutive perfect replays; Mirror Mode and muted-node clears award extra bonus, and the best run posts to a simple.1
tips: Listen for pitch steps as much as light position; audio tells you the route faster than visuals alone.
Use Mirror Mode only when the pattern feels chunked into pairs or symmetric shapes.
If a red desynced node appears late in the chain, repair only if the timer margin is safe; otherwise trust visual memory and protect the run
